degree: Bachelor of Science in Architectural Engineering

sections.title: Degree Requirements
sections.notes: Minimum of 136 credits is required for a Bachelor of Science in Architectural Engineering degree. Students must have a minimum CGPA of 2.00

sections.title: Core Course Policy
sections.notes:
A student must earn a minimum grade of C- in all AE and CVL core courses (even when enrolling in the course as a prerequisite). The core courses are the following CVL 203; CVL 231; CVL 270; CVL 292; CVL 297; LA 250; LA 255; CVL 341; CVL343; CVL 398; CGT 164; COM 114; MA 261; MA 265; MA 266; PHYS 241; STAT 511; AE 200; AE 310; AE 311, AE 413; AE 414; AE 415; AE 498; AE 513; AE 514; AE 515; AE 516; AE 522.
AE 498 (Senior Project Course) must be taken in the last semester. The only exception to this rule is when students plan to graduate during a summer semester. In this case, they may take AE 498 during the prior spring semester.

sections.title: General Engineering Requirements
sections.notes: It is expected that CGT 164 will cover technical sketching and CAD software, necessary for engineering construction documents.

sections.title: Basic Science Selective
sections.notes: Choice of one 100-level course from BIOL or EAPS.

sections.title: Science Selective
sections.notes: Choose one of the following (4) CHM 116 or (3) CS 159.

sections.title: General Education Requirements
sections.notes:
Architectural Engineering students must select AD 454 as one of their GEE courses.
Courses must be drawn from the following General Education areas at AUM Speech and Communication, English Language and Literature, History, Fine Arts, Physical Education, Self-Development and Life Style, Culinary Arts, Ethics, Social Sciences, Psychological Sciences, Natural Sciences, Child Development and Family Studies, Economics, Philosophy, Other Languages, and Science, Technology and Society (please refer to Course Catalogue, General Education section).
In order to ensure sufficient exposure to General Education topics, unless otherwise specified by the degree requirements of the academic major, the student cannot take more than 2 courses from the same areasub-area as, shown in tables below

Cannot be considered as a General Education course;
Do not count towards the 2-course limit in General Education requirement.
